---
title: "Test Tiers, Canon Parity, and Package Scope"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

**Purpose:** Explain the four test tiers (CRAN / fast / smoke / full), what
"canonical parity" means (R output matches MegaODA.exe / CTA.exe on covered
fixtures), which fixtures are currently green, and what remains future scope.

**Eval policy:** Entirely narrative. No fits, no computations.

---

*This article is not yet expanded. Placeholder sections are listed below.*

## Test tiers

| Tier | Variable | When to use |
|------|----------|-------------|
| CRAN (default) | unset | CRAN submission and default `devtools::check()` |
| fast | `ODACORE_TEST_TIER=fast` | Development loop; same as CRAN but explicit |
| smoke | `ODACORE_TEST_TIER=smoke` | Required before CTA / MDSA / graphics commits |
| full | `ODACORE_TEST_TIER=full` | Release gate and deep canon parity work only |

## Canonical parity: what it means

## Currently green fixtures

## Current scope: binary CTA only

## Future scope

### `predict.oda_fit()` / `summary.oda_fit()` — implemented

### Balance diagnostics — deferred

### Multiclass CTA — not yet scoped

### Full SDA / auto_SDA — not yet scoped

### Political affiliation example — data unavailable
